lede: A look at August 15 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Juanlu Sánchez and Brendan Hansen, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Donald Trump
events.desc: US president Donald Trump meets with Russian president Vladimir Putin in Alaska, the first such summit since the 2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine.

events.title: Free Aceh Movement
events.desc: The Helsinki Agreement between the Free Aceh Movement and the Government of Indonesia was signed, ending almost three decades of fighting.

events.title: Ohio State University Radio Observatory
events.desc: The Big Ear, a radio telescope operated by Ohio State University as part of the SETI project, receives a radio signal from deep space; the event is named the "Wow! signal" from the notation made by a volunteer on the project.

events.title: Fulbert Youlou
events.desc: President Fulbert Youlou is overthrown in the Republic of the Congo, after a three-day uprising in the capital.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Operation Pedestal: The oil tanker SS Ohio reaches the island of Malta barely afloat carrying vital fuel supplies for the island's defenses.

events.title: Josef Jakobs
events.desc: Corporal Josef Jakobs is executed by firing squad for espionage at the Tower of London at 07:12, making him the last person to be executed at the Tower.

events.title: War of 1812
events.desc: War of 1812: British forces launch a costly and unsuccessful night attack against American defenders during the siege of Fort Erie.

events.title: Michael VIII Palaiologos
events.desc: Michael VIII Palaiologos is crowned as the first Byzantine emperor in fifty-seven years.

events.title: Stephen I of Hungary
events.desc: King Stephen I, the first king of Hungary, dies; his nephew, Peter Orseolo, succeeds him.

events.title: Eustathios Daphnomeles
events.desc: Byzantine general Eustathios Daphnomeles blinds and captures Ibatzes of Bulgaria by a ruse, thereby ending Bulgarian resistance against Emperor Basil II's conquest of Bulgaria.

births.name: Jim Dale
births.line: English actor, narrator, singer, director, and composer

deaths.text: Peter Marshall (entertainer), American game show host, performer, and singer
Euclides da Cunha, Brazilian sociologist and journalist
Robert de Sorbon, French theologian and educator, founded the College of Sorbonne

observances.text: Christian feast day: Feast day of the Assumption of Mary, one of the Catholic holy days of obligation (a public holiday in Austria, Belgium, Benin, Bosnia, Burundi, Cameroon, Chile, Colombia, Croatia, Cyprus, France, some states in Germany, Greece, Guatemala, Italy, Ivory Coast, Lebanon, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Madagascar, Malta, Mauritius, Paraguay, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Senegal, Seychelles, Slovenia, Spain, Switzerland, Togo, and Vanuatu); and its related observances: Feast of the Dormition of the Theotokos (Eastern Orthodox, Oriental Orthodox and Eastern Catholic Churches)
